column1: // Golden cases for ksqlparser (STEP 2: ksql). Format: <input><TAB><expected AST>.
// Lines beginning with // are comments; blank lines are ignored.
// Expected value @ABORT means the input must make the parser exit nonzero.
// A query desugars to the t[E] application (`verb; t; c; b; a) with
// t=from c=where b=by a=phrases. Absent clauses are the empty list ().
// ---- select: clauses present/absent ----
select from t
select a from t
select a,b from t
select a by c from t
select a from t where d
select a by c from t where d
// ---- where conditions are ordinary expressions ----
select a from t where d>0
select a from t where f=c,h>5
// ---- by and select are comma-separated phrase lists ----
select a,b by f,g from t
// ---- aliases are the existing `:` assignment shape (no new syntax) ----
select qty:sum amt from t
update qty:sum amt from t
select p:sum i,q:avg j by f,g from t where f=c,h>5
// ---- exec, update, delete share the (verb; t; c; b; a) shape ----
exec a from t
update a:x+1 from t
delete from t where d
delete a,b from t
// ---- a query is a noun: it composes and nests ----
count select a from t
select a from (select b from t) where d
// ---- inside ()/[]/{}, comma is the join verb again, not a separator ----
select (a,b) from t
// ---- the , separator is only the dyadic join; monadic enlist ,: is a verb ----
select ,:a from t
// ---- from-expr is a full expression: , joins there, and it stops at where ----
select a from t lj u
select from t lj `k key u where d
select a from t,u
select a,b from +`a`b!,:'1 2
// ---- keywords are reserved only positionally: still names elsewhere ----
from
from:3
where+1
// ---- malformed: a clause keyword must carry its operand ----
select a
select a by c
select x,y from
select x by from t
select x from t where
// ---- malformed: clauses out of order leave a token trailing the query ----
select a,b from t,u by a=2
select a from t by b
select a from t where d by e
